title: Weak-type endpoint bounds for a class of bilinear Hilbert-transform operators
abstract: We establish weak-type $(1,1)$ endpoint estimates for a class of bilinear Hilbert-transform operators arising in time-frequency analysis, extending the strong-type results of Lacey–Thiele.
ai_proof_review:
## Setup

A tightening of the known $L^p$ theory at the endpoint. Uses time-frequency tile decompositions and a Calderón–Zygmund argument adapted to the bilinear setting.

## Section 5 (weak-type estimate)

The weak-$(1,1)$ estimate is established via a careful selection of bad tiles and their geometric arrangement. The constants are explicit and the argument is essentially mechanical given Lacey–Thiele's framework. I checked the key inequality on p. 23; it goes through.

## Overall

A solid extension. Will be useful for the harmonic-analysis crowd working on multilinear operators.
